events.description: Patents issued to patentees, largely mixed-blood element, many disposed of land through mortgages
341 patents-in-fee issued to persons with less than one-half Indian blood
370 patents-in-fee issued to Indians of half or less Indian blood
Patents issued under Declaration of Policy 1919
Children allotted land 50-70 miles from parents' homes along water courses
Children allotted land 50-70 miles from parents' homes
James B. Kitoh transferred to Fort Peck Reservation from Standing Rock after six years
Special detail to purchase cattle for Indians in the southwest prior to transfer
County authorities assessed ceded lands as farm lands despite being grazing lands, causing high taxes

financial_transactions.description: Sale of patented lands by Indians; 90% at Shoshone, 45 of 57 at Whitehouse District, many at Standing Rock and Pine Ridge

financial_transactions.amount: half to two-thirds of land value
financial_transactions.description: Mortgages placed on patented lands, interest and principal ate up resources leading to foreclosure
Leasing of patented lands by Indians who preferred not to farm, though farming would yield greater returns
Tax payments on ceded portions assessed as farm lands rather than grazing lands
Purchase of cattle, implements, and home improvements by some Indians using sale proceeds wisely
Purchase of automobiles and expenditures on riotous living by others who sold lands

financial_transactions.amount: twenty to thirty dollars per acre
financial_transactions.description: Sales of valuable agricultural land by young men and women who had no use for land far from homes

relationships.subject: James B. Kitoh
relationships.context: Transferred to Fort Peck as Superintendent

relationships.subject: James B. Kitoh
relationships.context: Served as Superintendent for six years prior to transfer

relationships.subject: Mixed-blood women
relationships.context: Many mixed-blood Indian women married to white men immediately disposed of their lands

relationships.subject: Children of heads of families
relationships.context: Allotted land 50-70 miles from parents' homes in 1908-1909, forced to take allotments inland from water courses

relationships.subject: Wives of heads of families
relationships.context: Allotted 320 acres under Amendatory Act, often not adjoining husbands' allotments

relationships.subject: Returned soldiers
relationships.context: Issued patents under Enabling Act of 1905, many retained lands and did well

fee_patents.context: Less than one-half Indian blood at Pine Ridge
Half or less Indian blood at Pine Ridge
Shoshone Reservation irrigable agricultural lands; blood groups included Full-blood, Three-fourths, Five-eighths, One-half, Three-eighths, One-fourth, Five-sixteenths, One-eighth
Standing Rock Reservation, largely mixed-blood element

correspondence.subject: Fee patent policy and circular letter requesting information
correspondence.action_requested: Report on fee patent Indians and policy recommendations
correspondence.outcome: Multiple responses received from various agencies

correspondence.subject: Response to circular letter regarding Standing Rock and Fort Peck
correspondence.outcome: Provided information on patentees at Standing Rock, noted transfer to Fort Peck

correspondence.subject: Report on fee patent policy at Cheyenne River
correspondence.outcome: Recommended continued policy with individual merit consideration, not class distinction

correspondence.subject: Response regarding fee patent Indians
correspondence.outcome: Reported 45 of 57 citizen Indians in Whitehouse District sold lands, 12 holding

correspondence.subject: Response regarding fee patent policy
correspondence.outcome: Criticized rapid release, recommended individual examination and holding 20-40 acres

correspondence.subject: Response to letter of March 31st regarding fee patents
correspondence.outcome: Reported on Pine Ridge patentees, opposed indiscriminate policy, noted 10% in debt and 15% with mortgages

correspondence.subject: Report on fee patent Indians at Pine Ridge
correspondence.outcome: Recommended holding 160 acres in trust, selling surplus under supervision

correspondence.subject: Report on fee patent policy
correspondence.outcome: Recommended withholding patents due to low land prices not equal to appraised valuation

correspondence.subject: Report on fee patent Indians
correspondence.outcome: Recommended individual examination, cited Leroy Cuny and William Twiss as examples

correspondence.subject: Report on fee patent policy
correspondence.outcome: Recommended releasing young educated Indians, holding lands for old and afflicted

correspondence.subject: Report on fee patent Indians
correspondence.outcome: Recommended individual examination based on character of land and Indian

correspondence.subject: Report on fee patent policy
correspondence.outcome: Recommended individual examination by superintendent and farmers

correspondence.subject: Report on 303 patents at Shoshone Reservation
correspondence.outcome: Reported 90% sold or mortgaged, recommended holding 160 acres as not adapted for farming

legislative_actions.outcome: Allowed returned soldiers to receive patents-in-fee and became homes for Indians
Allowed allotment of children in 1908-1909 and wives of heads of families (320 acres)

legislative_actions.bill_number: Twenty-five year holding period with ten year extension
legislative_actions.outcome: Original restriction period before patent issuance, extendable by ten years if not competent
